{"query":"FAA Airspace Modernization office new air traffic control system installation status 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1539,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://fedscoop.com/dot-faa-atc-modernization-progress-next-funding/","title":"FAA head details air traffic control modernization progress, next steps | FedScoop","content":"“It’s on everyone’s mind, on this committee and frankly within this administration, that modernization not turn into another NextGen boondoggle,” Bedford said Tuesday during a House hearing that marked his first appearance before Congress since being sworn into office in July.
Bedford was the lone witness in two hearings this week, detailing the state of American aviation to members of a House committee Tuesday and updating senators on the air traffic control modernization initiative Wednesday.
The FAA will have committed about half of its $12.5 billion budget earmarked for modernizing its air traffic control system by the end of the year, according to Bedford.
The modernization program is expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2027."},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/marisagarcia/2026/05/25/you-can-now-track-faa-efforts-to-fix-antiquated-air-traffic-control/","title":"You Can Now Track FAA Efforts To Fix ‘Antiquated’ Air Traffic Control","content":"Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy holds a news conference about adding more air traffic controllers to the workforce at the DOT headquarters on May 01, 2025 in Washington, DC.
Counter to the Trump Administration's historic layoffs of federal workers, Duffy announced incentives to train new air traffic controllers and to retain controllers that may be close to retirement.
Now, as proof of progress, the Federal Aviation Administration has launched a new website where travellers can track how far along the program to modernise air traffic control is, nationally and locally.
The U.S. has been contending with an ongoing shortage of trained air traffic controllers, with staffing levels lagging after a series of government shutdowns that left air traffic controllers unpaid.
We’re taking a different approach – letting every American get a front row view of how President Trump is revolutionizing our skies,” Duffy said in the FAA’s announcement on Friday.
The air traffic control modernization program will replace core infrastructure, including radar, software, hardware, and telecommunications equipment, at over 4,600 sites across the country.
200 airports with new Surface Awareness Initiative surveillance technology
Progress chart of projects published to the FAA's new Modern Skies website as of May 25.
The FAA says the wide-ranging air traffic control modernization program will take over 10 million labor hours or 1.5 million working days to complete."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/fedscoop_the-federal-aviation-administration-is-making-activity-7452720394194698240-gxtw","title":"FAA Modernizes Air Traffic Control Systems | FedScoop posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"8597, introduced in the 119th Congress on April 30, 2026, aims to mandate the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration to procure and implement certified airborne position reference tools at air traffic control towers.
Last Wednesday I had the pleasure of attending the Zoom meeting with the RDU Airport Authority to discuss the proposed airspace changes around RDU, how it would increase safety around the airport, and the potential implications for airliners as well as GA pilots.
The Federal Aviation Administration is hosting a virtual public meeting on Wednesday, May 6 about proposed changes to the airspace around RDU.
Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy and Federal Aviation Administration Administrator Bryan Bedford outlined a vision for improved air traffic management at an April event in Washington."},{"url":"https://www.wfmd.com/2026/05/15/faa-unveils-new-air-traffic-controller-hiring-plan-after-chief-warned-system-was-chronically-understaffed/","title":"FAA unveils new air traffic controller hiring plan after chief ...","content":"May 15, 2026 — The agency plans to hire 2,200 new air traffic controllers in fiscal year 2026, 2,300 in fiscal year 2027 and 2,400 in fiscal year 2028"}]}

A **lock** ( LockA locked padlock ) or **https://** means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. ## Subnav: Aircraft 1. ## Subnav: Air Traffic 1. ## Subnav: Airports 1. ## Subnav: Airmen 1. ## Subnav: Data & Research 1. # FAA Releases Bold, New Air Traffic Controller Hiring Plan. **WASHINGTON** – The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) today released a bold, new air traffic controller workforce plan that will erase the longstanding staffing shortage, prepare for future demand and ensure the long-term safety and operational efficiency of the National Airspace System (NAS). The FAA’s 2026 Air Traffic Controller Workforce Plan  is based on three strategic pillars: expanding the agency’s aggressive and successful supercharged hiring; optimizing controller efficiency; and modernizing the NAS.","score":0.5803764,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.govexec.com/management/2026/04/transportation-air-traffic-control-overhaul/413024","title":"Transportation touts air traffic control modernization, presses Congress ...","content":"DOT says its scaled-back modernization is on track, but needs billions more to finish, as lawmakers","score":0.5008063,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/avweek/posts/faas-plan-for-its-new-air-traffic-management-software-sees-the-initial-operation/1461944722643426","title":"AVIATION WEEK - FAA’s plan for its new air traffic...","content":"## AVIATION WEEK's post. ### **AVIATION WEEK**. 17 June at 07:19  ·. FAA Expands On New ATC Software Deployment Details | Aviation Week.","score":0.40330887,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://fedscoop.com/dot-faa-atc-modernization-progress-next-funding","title":"FAA head details air traffic control modernization progress, next steps | FedScoop","content":"With modernization efforts ramping up at the Transportation Department’s Federal Aviation Administration, leaders have a sharpened focus on efficiency, safety and accountability, Administrator Bryan Bedford told Congress this week. “It’s on everyone’s mind, on this committee and frankly within this administration, that modernization not turn into another NextGen boondoggle,” Bedford said Tuesday during a House hearing that marked his first appearance before Congress since being sworn into office in July. Bedford was the lone witness in two hearings this week, detailing the state of American aviation to members of a House committee Tuesday and updating senators on the air traffic control modernization initiative Wednesday. The FAA will have committed about half of its $12.5 billion budget earmarked for modernizing its air traffic control system by the end of the year, according to Bedford. The FAA also plans to close a radar acquisition by the end of the year. Peraton “put the entire [Department of Defense] in the cloud, a cyber-secured cloud,” Bedford told the House committee.","score":0.39754128,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://modernskies.faa.gov","title":"Modern Skies | A New Era of American Aviation","content":"A New Era of American Aviation.
